Chapter 01 · Declarations

A careful, rational grammar for pages that expect to be read.

Set on a twelve-column grid with 24 pixel gutters. One ink, one paper, a single moment of red. No shadows. No gradients. No rounded corners except on the small status pills at the foot of the page.

On restraint

Restraint is not the absence of decision. It is the presence of every decision made more slowly than usual, by a reader who owes the page nothing.
